vBRIEFInfo.description: Adoption blocker: deft/run doctor passes green on a machine without uv installed, then every subsequent task command fails with `uv: command not found`. Plus expected_dirs hardcodes pre-v0.20 dirs that no longer exist, producing spurious 'Missing directory' warnings on every clean checkout.

plan.title: cmd_doctor: add uv check; replace pre-v0.20 expected_dirs with v0.20+ layout

narratives.Problem: Two defects in run::cmd_doctor (run lines 2563-2612). (1) Missing uv check: every deft task script runs via `uv run python ...` (tasks/migrate.yml:34, tasks/issue.yml:27, etc.), making uv a hard runtime dependency. cmd_doctor checks task / git / python3 / go / node but not uv, so a fresh-machine user gets a green report and then an opaque `uv: command not found` failure as soon as they run task migrate:vbrief. (2) Stale expected_dirs: the list at run line 2596 includes pre-v0.20 directories core/, interfaces/, tools/, swarm/, meta/ that were removed during the vBRIEF cutover. On a clean v0.23 checkout cmd_doctor warns 'Missing directory: <name>/' for each, burying any real signal. The agent-driven setup skill already implements the right uv check (skills/deft-directive-setup/SKILL.md::Environment Preflight); cmd_doctor must mirror it.
narratives.Overview: Three changes in run::cmd_doctor: (1) add `check_command("uv", "uv (Astral Python runner)", required=True)` and surface https://docs.astral.sh/uv/ as the install pointer; (2) replace expected_dirs with the v0.20+ canonical layout (languages, strategies, skills, templates, tasks, scripts, vbrief); (3) consolidate the warning summary so missing-uv is not buried under spurious dir warnings. Add tests covering the uv-missing path and the no-spurious-warnings path.
narratives.Background: cmd_doctor predates the v0.20 vBRIEF cutover; expected_dirs was never updated when core/, interfaces/, tools/, swarm/ were retired. The agent path was instrumented for uv (#566 era) but cmd_doctor was not.
narratives.Constraint: MUST NOT regress existing checks (task / git / python3 / go / node). MUST keep cmd_doctor non-fatal for optional tools (go, node) while making uv required. SHOULD use the same install URL the agent skill points at for consistency. The expected_dirs replacement MUST be verified against the actual v0.23 checkout to avoid swapping one stale list for another.
narratives.Risk: Low. cmd_doctor is read-only diagnostic. The only behavioral risk is making uv-missing a hard error on machines that legitimately don't have uv (e.g. a user only ever invoking `deft/run bootstrap` which is pure-Python). Mitigation: keep uv 'required' but distinguish the exit code so existing CI invocations that ignore warnings still see a clear error rather than silent green.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) run doctor on a machine without uv exits non-zero with a clear install-pointer message; (2) run doctor on a clean v0.23 checkout reports zero spurious 'Missing directory' warnings; (3) two new tests cover those paths; (4) main.md / docs reference the install URL consistently.
narratives.Test: tests/cli/test_doctor_uv.py (new): mock shutil.which('uv') -> None, run cmd_doctor, assert error path printed and non-zero return. tests/cli/test_doctor_dirs.py (new): run cmd_doctor against the live framework checkout (use a fixture pointing get_script_dir at a known-good tree), assert zero 'Missing directory' warnings.
narratives.Action: 1. Edit run::cmd_doctor: add uv check, replace expected_dirs. 2. Add the two new tests. 3. Run task check. 4. CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased] / Fixed.
narratives.Urgency: Adoption blocker for first-time CLI-first users on Windows / fresh containers / CI runners. Same severity as #791 but on a different surface.

items.title: Add uv check to cmd_doctor; mark required; print install URL on miss

narrative.Action: In run::cmd_doctor (~line 2585) add `check_command("uv", "uv (Astral Python runner)", required=True)`. On miss, the existing error() helper should print 'uv not found - required; install: https://docs.astral.sh/uv/ or `pip install uv`'. Mirror the install pointer used in skills/deft-directive-setup/SKILL.md line 57.

items.title: Replace expected_dirs with v0.20+ canonical layout

narrative.Action: At run line 2596, replace `expected_dirs = ["core", "languages", "interfaces", "tools", "swarm", "templates", "meta"]` with the verified current set. Run `Get-ChildItem -Directory C:\repos\Deft\directive` (or `ls -d */`) on a clean checkout to derive the truth before editing. Suggested set: languages, strategies, skills, templates, tasks, scripts, vbrief. Drop the legacy entries.

items.title: Consolidate doctor summary so missing-uv is surfaced first

narrative.Action: Group findings by severity (errors first, then warnings) so a missing-uv 'required' message renders above optional-tool warnings. Keep the existing success path unchanged when nothing is wrong.

Use unittest.mock.patch on shutil.which to return None for 'uv'. Invoke cmd_doctor via the existing test harness. Assert non-zero return / error message contains 'uv'. Pin to existing pytest fixtures.
Run cmd_doctor against the real framework root via subprocess. Capture stdout. Assert no line matches 'Missing directory:'. This locks the v0.20+ layout truth into a regression test.
Two-bullet entry: 'fix(doctor): require uv and surface https://docs.astral.sh/uv/ when missing (#792)' and 'fix(doctor): replace pre-v0.20 expected_dirs with current canonical layout (#792)'.

items.title: Run task check and confirm clean

narrative.Action: Pre-PR gate. Two new test files satisfy forward-coverage rule from main.md.

x-tracking.estimated_size: M (1 file edit + 2 new tests + CHANGELOG)
